file_name: Microgenics Corporation - Collaborative Development and Commercialization Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.7 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" shall mean efforts and resources normally utilized by a Party for a product owned by it or to which it has rights, which is of similar market potential at a similar stage in its product life, taking into account the competitiveness of the marketplace, the proprietary position of the product, the regulatory structure involved, the profitability of the applicable products, the relative benefit that accrues to actual and potential patients and other relevant factors; provided, that, in any event, "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" under this Agreement require that a Party (a) [***], (b) [***], and (c) [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" shall mean efforts and resources that the party deems reasonable for a product owned by it or to which it has rights, without considering market potential, product lifecycle, marketplace competitiveness, product position, regulatory structure, product profitability, or benefits to patients. The term requires only minimal actions, reflecting a low bar for fulfilling obligations.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by redefining "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" to mean only what the party considers reasonable, explicitly disregarding factors previously required. This contradicts the implied due diligence and standard of effort expected in legal agreements, potentially allowing a party to underperform their obligations without recourse. The original definition suggests effort and resources normally utilized for a similar product, while the changed text introduces a much lower bar for compliance. The contradiction arises in multiple sections, specifically where 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' are stipulated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Party Responsibilities. 2.2.1 General. Microgenics and Achaogen shall each perform their respective obligations under the Research Program, using Commercially Reasonable Efforts, in accordance with the Project Plan and in compliance with Applicable Law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to disregard crucial factors like market potential and product lifecycle, explicitly contradicting the expectation in section 2.2.1 that Microgenics and Achaogen fulfill obligations using 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts'. This creates a direct conflict because the changed definition allows for minimal effort, whereas 2.2.1 implies a higher standard.

perturbation.original_text: 1.8 "Confidential Information" shall mean all proprietary and confidential business, technical, scientific, and/or regulatory information relating to the Assay, Plazomicin, and/or the purpose of, or activities under, this Agreement, that is provided by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party to a Receiving Party hereunder, whether disclosed in writing or orally.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.8 "Confidential Information" shall mean only information explicitly marked as "Confidential" by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure. Any information not so marked is deemed non-confidential, regardless of its nature or the circumstances of its disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting 'Confidential Information' to only what is explicitly marked as such, this change contradicts the original definition's broader scope, which included all proprietary business, technical, scientific, and regulatory information. The in-text contradiction undermines the protection of information not explicitly labeled, creating uncertainty and potential misuse of data. This contradicts sections detailing information exchange and regulatory compliance, where confidential data is shared without the explicit marking requirement introduced by this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12 Confidentiality 12.1 Nondisclosure. During the Term, and for a period of [***] ([***]) years thereafter, all Confidential Information disclosed to a Party hereto or its Affiliates (the "Receiving Party") by the other Party or its Affiliates (the "Disclosing Party") shall be deemed confidential and shall be treated as such by the Receiving Party (meaning that the Receiving Party shall take the same steps to protect such information as it does to protect its own confidential information, which in any event shall be no less than the reasonable protective measures for the industry) and shall only be used for the purposes of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information shall not include information that is: (a) known by the Receiving Party at the time of its receipt and not through a prior disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (b) at the time of disclosure or thereafter, becomes published or otherwise part of the public domain through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) subsequently disclosed to the Receiving Party by a Third Party having the right to make such a disclosure; or (d) developed by the Receiving Party, as evidenced by its records, independently of information received by it from the Disclosing Party hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 1.8 significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only explicitly marked information, directly contradicting section 12.1 which treats 'all Confidential Information disclosed' as confidential, regardless of whether it's marked. This creates a clear conflict because under the changed definition, much information that would have been considered confidential under section 12.1 is now not, potentially undermining the non-disclosure obligations. The contradiction is strong because it alters a core definition used throughout the agreement, leading to uncertainty and potential breach of contract due to inconsistent application of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: 1.28 "Shortfall License" shall mean a temporary, fully-paid up, royalty-free, worldwide non-exclusive license granted by Microgenics (including on behalf of its Affiliates as appropriate), under Section 4.3.2.3, to (a) the Immunoassay Technologies and (b) all other intellectual property rights (including Patent applications, Patents, trade secrets, copyrights, and trademarks) (i) of Microgenics (or its Affiliates as appropriate) arising out of the performance of this Agreement or the Antibody Development Agreement, or (ii) Controlled by Microgenics (or its Affiliates as appropriate) that are necessary or desirable for or used in the manufacture and commercialization of the Assay, which license would be for the manufacture, use, sale, offer for sale and import of the Assay. Such license shall be fully sub-licensable to any Third Party for purposes of manufacturing and commercializing the Assay (including the components thereof) under Section 4.3.2.3.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.28 "Shortfall License" shall mean a license granted at Microgenics' sole discretion, based on factors such as their current financial status, future projections, and relationship with Achaogen at the time of the supply shortfall. There is no guarantee a license will be granted.
perturbation.explanation: The change to the definition of 'Shortfall License' introduces a contradiction by making the grant of the license discretionary, contingent on Microgenics' evaluation of their circumstances. This conflicts with the original definition that implies an automatic granting of the license upon a supply shortfall. This change contradicts the purpose of Shortfall License, which originally meant to act as an obligatory measure in such a situation and that can lead to disputes over supply obligations, affecting Achaogen's ability to commercialize Plazomicin. In addition, section 4.3.2.3 is directly affected by this change because it indicates a situation where the Shortfall License, should be granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3.2.3 If, for any reason, [***], both Microgenics and the Back-up Supplier (as applicable) are unable to supply the Assay in quantities sufficient to meet the quantities of the Assay either (a) as set forth in each applicable Binding Forecast or (b) based on such other measure of commercial demand as agreed to by the Parties in writing, in either case, during any given [***] ([***]) month period for a given country in the Territory, then Microgenics hereby grants Achaogen or its designee a Shortfall License, which license shall survive until [***] ([***]) months following the date that either Microgenics or the Back-up Supplier (as applicable) is in a position again to fulfill such demand (as such positioning is demonstrated [***]) (the "Supply Resumption Date"). The Shortfall License shall expire automatically [***] ([***]) months after the Supply Resumption Date; provided, however, that (a) Achaogen shall be entitled to use, sell, offer for sale, and import any assay that are in inventory prior to the expiration of the Shortfall License (even if such activity occurs after expiration of the Shortfall License). Additionally, [***], Microgenics shall (i) make its personnel available for a reasonable period of time (not to exceed [***] ([***]) months) to effect a successful technology transfer with respect to the manufacture and commercialization of such Assay under the terms of this paragraph, (ii) provide Achaogen with copies of the physical embodiment of all processes, protocols, procedures, methods, tests and other intellectual property rights licensed to Achaogen under the Shortfall License, as applicable, related to Assay (including the manufacture thereof), (iii) supply [***] (including [***]) reasonably required to perform [***] as may be required by the applicable Regulatory Authorities, and upon request by Achaogen, [***], provide Achaogen (and/or its designee) with [***] related to the Assay, and (iv) promptly assist Achaogen (and/or its designee) in obtaining all necessary Regulatory Approvals and/or modifying and/or transferring existing Regulatory Approvals to enable Achaogen (and/or its designee) to develop, make, have made, use, market, distribute, import, sell and offer for sale the Assay ([***]).
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Shortfall License" makes it discretionary and based on Microgenics' evaluation, directly contradicting section 4.3.2.3, which mandates the granting of a Shortfall License if Microgenics and the Back-up Supplier cannot meet demand. The original text defines the Shortfall License as obligatory under specific supply circumstances, while the changed text removes that obligation, creating a direct conflict. This contradiction affects the enforceability of Achaogen's supply rights under 4.3.2.3.
